name: 7-Hydroxymitragynine

effects: Discussed as a potent kratom alkaloid with opioid-receptor-mediated analgesic and dependence-related risk signals.

interactions: Likely additive CNS/respiratory depression with opioids, alcohol, or sedative-hypnotics.

name: Aconine
description: Aconine is an Aconitum-related diterpenoid alkaloid generally discussed within aconitum toxicology rather than therapeutic pharmacology. Direct human evidence is limited.
effects: Aconine is a hydrolysis-related aconitum alkaloid discussed mainly in toxicity context.
Compared with aconitine, available evidence remains sparse and largely non-clinical.

interactions: Compound-specific interaction evidence is limited; class-based cardiotoxic risk warrants caution with antiarrhythmic-active drugs.

name: Aconitine
description: Aconitine is a highly toxic diterpenoid alkaloid from Aconitum species and is primarily characterized by poisoning literature rather than therapeutic trials.
effects: Human poisoning reports consistently describe severe cardiotoxic and neurotoxic presentations.
Forensic and toxicology literature supports high acute toxicity risk.
contraindications: Contraindicated for unsupervised internal use due to risk of life-threatening arrhythmia and neurologic toxicity.
interactions: Likely dangerous with other agents that alter cardiac conduction or depress respiration/CNS function.

name: CBD
description: Cannabidiol (CBD) is a non-intoxicating phytocannabinoid with approved use in specific seizure disorders and broad off-label consumer exposure. Human reviews support a measurable adverse-event and interaction profile.
effects: Clinical and post-marketing syntheses report antiseizure use with common adverse effects including somnolence and gastrointestinal symptoms.
Human safety reviews identify transaminase elevations in some populations, especially with interacting drugs.
contraindications: Avoid unsupervised use in pregnancy or severe hepatic impairment due to limited safety certainty and interaction complexity.
Use caution in patients taking centrally acting sedatives or hepatotoxic co-therapies.
interactions: CBD can inhibit CYP-mediated metabolism and increase exposure to selected co-medications; structured medication review is recommended.

name: Luteolin
description: Luteolin is a dietary flavone studied for anti-inflammatory and redox-active pharmacology. Evidence includes mechanistic studies and translational safety reviews with potential drug-interaction liability.
effects: In human and translational reviews, luteolin is discussed for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant pathway modulation.
Mechanistic literature reports modulation of CYP enzymes and transporters, which may alter co-administered drug exposure.
contraindications: Use caution with narrow-therapeutic-index drugs because CYP/transporter modulation may change exposure.
Evidence for efficacy is mixed and largely translational; avoid treating as a substitute for standard care.
interactions: Potential interaction risk with CYP-metabolized medicines and transporter substrates; monitor for altered drug response.

sources.title: Drug interaction and safety implications of luteolin supplementation: translational review.
Luteolin modulation of CYP and transporter pathways: integrated mechanistic in vitro and in vivo analysis.
